citations.description: show_ticket returned 'comments': [] — no recorded live-run outcome or attestation in the ticket.

findings.criterion: `pytest tests/integration/test_reconcile_live_e2e.py` (all 12, serial) passes repeatedly against the live test project (no `never became index-visible`).
findings.detail: This criterion requires a live Jira run to verify. The ticket has no comments and no recorded attestation of a successful live run (comments array is empty). There is no evidence in the repository or ticket that this was exercised and confirmed against the real Jira test project.
findings.remediation: Run `pytest tests/integration/test_reconcile_live_e2e.py` (all 12, serial) with live Jira credentials and record the result as a ticket comment including: the run timestamp, the Jira project (e.g. REB), observed test outcomes (all 12 pass, no 'never became index-visible' errors), and any specific issue keys created/deleted during the run.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
